privileged_info_summary: Three high-altitude Colorado airports verified against FAA/AirNav.com data: KASE (Aspen-Pitkin County) at 7,837.9 ft elevation with 8,006 ft runway and 118.85 MHz CTAF; KTEX (Telluride Regional) at 9,069.7 ft elevation with 7,111 ft runway and 123.0 MHz CTAF (metadata incorrectly claims 122.8 MHz); KEGE (Eagle County Regional) at 6,547.4 ft elevation with 9,000 ft runway and 119.8 MHz CTAF. All three airports are controlled by Denver ARTCC. Data is real, from public FAA sources.
